channel.title: SevenAtSevenSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: At least 6,000 killed over 3 days during RSF attack on Sudan's el-Fasher, UN says
item.description: More than 6,000 people were killed in over three days when a Sudanese paramilitary group unleashed "a wave of intense violence" in Sudan's Darfur regi...

item.title: Obama responds to Trump sharing racist AI video depicting him as an ape
item.description: "There doesn't seem to be any shame about this among people who used to feel like you had to have some sort of decorum," Obama said in an interview th...

item.title: Trump's border czar says a 'small' security force will stay in Minnesota
item.description: Tom Homan says this federal force will stay "for a short period of time" to protect immigration agents who remain as the sweeping crackdown draws down...

item.title: Rockstar athletes like Ilia Malinin often get 'the yips' at the Olympics. It can make them stronger
item.description: Ilia Malinin's painful falls at the Milan Cortina Games follow in a long tradition of great U.S. athletes who get the "yips" or the "twisties" during ...

item.title: Photos: The flying doctors of Lesotho won't let their wings be clipped
item.description: This band of airborne health workers bring essential medical care to isolated communities in the southern African nation. In addition to turbulence, t...

item.title: FBI: DNA recovered from glove found near Guthrie home that appears to match glove worn by suspect
item.description: The FBI says a glove containing DNA was found about two miles from Nancy Guthrie’s Arizona home and appears to match those worn by a masked person out...

item.title: India has 100M weekly active ChatGPT users, Sam Altman says
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman says India has the largest number of student users of ChatGPT worldwide.

item.title: Longtime NPR host David Greene sues Google over NotebookLM voice
item.description: The longtime host of NPR’s “Morning Edition,” is suing Google, alleging that the male podcast voice in the company’s NotebookLM tool is based on him.

item.title: Anthropic and the Pentagon are reportedly arguing over Claude usage
item.description: The apparent issue: whether Claude can be used for mass domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons.

item.title: No clear path to ending the partial government shutdown as lawmakers dig in over DHS oversight
Trump’s border czar says ‘small’ security force will remain in Minnesota after enforcement drawdown
The enterprise AI land grab is on. Glean is building the layer beneath the interface.
item.description: In this week's episode of the Equity podcast, Glean CEO Arvind Jain explains the company's shift from enterprise search tool to middleware layer for e...

item.title: OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger joins OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI said OpenClaw will live on as an open source project.

item.title: How to Buy Rental #2, #3, or #4 When You’re Out of Funds (Rookie Reply)
item.description: Stuck at one rental property? Maybe you spent years saving for that first down payment, and now, your funds are depleted. Where do you go from here? N...

item.title: How to (Legally) Pay the Least Amount in Taxes as a Real Estate Investor
item.description: This episode alone could save you hundreds, thousands, or tens of thousands in taxes—all with 100% legal means. If you own a rental property, you coul...

item.title: Grand Rapids Could Become a Boomtown as Investment Money Pours In
item.description: Looking for an affordable, cash-flowing market earmarked for major development and a projected population and economic surge? Grand Rapids, Michigan, ...

item.title: Buying (and Building) Houses Could Get a LOT Easier (New Bill)
item.description: This could be the most important shift in the housing market in years. Something truly remarkable just happened that will impact almost every facet of...

item.title: Over 60% of Buyers Bought Below Asking Price Last Year, With the Largest Discounts Since 2012
item.description: For most of us, the frenzied bidding wars and constant price hikes of the post-pandemic housing boom are recent memories. That’s why it might come as ...
